content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 26, 1993

William A. Carter
Assistant Secretary for Law
Enforcement and Counsel
California Environmental Protection Agency
555 Capitol Mall, Suite 235
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-93-038

Dear Mr. Carter:

This letter is in response to your request for informal
assistance¹ in conjunction with Mr. Michael Kenny regarding the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").²

You and Mr. Kenny, General Counsel for the Air Resources
Board (ARB), have asked us to review two letters and a memorandum
concerning the state contracting process and the application of
various conflicts of interest laws on employees of the ARB.³

The Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission")
does not have jurisdiction over the contracting practices of the
state. Thus, we cannot advise you whether the current practices
of the ARB are in compliance with the State Administrative Manual
(SAM), or whether you have correctly summarized laws other than
the Act in the materials you have submitted. However, we have
reviewed the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act summarized

_______________
¹ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

² Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

³ Furthermore, our advice is prospective in nature. We make no
comments regarding past conduct.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95812 ● (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-93-038
Page 2


in your memorandum dated November 6, 1992. We offer the following
suggestions with respect to your memorandum.


Disqualification and Disclosure

In general, the Act requires every public official to
disclose all his or her economic interests that could foreseeably
be affected by the exercise of the official's duties. (Section
87200-87313.) The Act, however, distinguishes from those persons
who must disclose all their economic interests (see, e.g.
Section 87200) and those who may have tailored disclosure
obligations.

Further, the disqualification requirements set out in Section
87103 differ from the reporting and disclosure requirements.
Therefore, we suggest you consider clarifying language in Page 2,
Paragraph 2 of the memorandum to reflect the distinction. For
example:

Section 87103 of the Act may require the
disqualification of public officials on the basis
of various direct and indirect effects on an
official's economic interests: (1) investments in
business entities, (2) interests in real property,
(3) sources of income, (4) positions held with
business organizations, and (5) gifts and their
sources.

The statements of economic interests filed by
public officials may be helpful in identifying
financial interests that may require
disqualification by the official. However, the
threshold requirements for disclosure differ from
disqualification. Therefore, it cannot be assumed
that a reportable interest automatically triggers
disqualification by an official. For example,
gifts of more than $50 are required to be reported.
(Section 87207.) However, gifts aggregating more
than $250 may require disqualification. (Section
87103.)


Informal Guidelines

Although informal guidelines are useful, you may also wish to
include a reference to Section 83114 in your memo which states any
person may request the Commission to provide written advice with
respect to the person's duties under the Act. As you may be
aware, only written advice from the Commission may confer immunity
from enforcement in an action arising out of the Political Reform
Act.

Our File No. A-93-038
Page 3


Also, please be advised that the Commission may be amending
Regulation 18700.1 which applies to "consultants" in the near
future.

I hope this letter is helpful to you. If you have any
further questions regarding this matter please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Jeff Marschner
General Counsel

Luisa Menchaca
Counsel, Legal Division

LM:docs:93038

sections.question_synthetic: How do the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act apply to employees of the Air Resources Board, specifically regarding the distinction between disqualification requirements and disclosure/reporting obligations?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC clarified that disqualification requirements under Section 87103 differ from reporting and disclosure requirements under Sections 87200-87313, noting that a reportable interest does not automatically trigger disqualification (e.g., gifts over $50 must be reported but gifts over $250 may require disqualification). The FPPC also recommended that informal guidelines reference Section 83114 regarding requests for written advice that provide immunity from enforcement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal advice (not opinion). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers, but content clearly identifies the legal inquiry and FPPC's guidance. File number format shows 1-93-038 and A-93-038 used interchangeably. Footnotes provide regulatory context.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How do the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act apply to employees of the Air Resources Board, specifically regarding the distinction between disqualification requirements and disclosure/reporting obligations?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC clarified that disqualification requirements under Section 87103 differ from reporting and disclosure requirements under Sections 87200-87313, noting that a reportable interest does not automatically trigger disqualification (e.g., gifts over $50 must be reported but gifts over $250 may require disqualification). The FPPC also recommended that informal guidelines reference Section 83114 regarding requests for written advice that provide immunity from enforcement.

embedding.summary: The FPPC provided informal assistance to the California Environmental Protection Agency and Air Resources Board regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, clarifying the distinction between disqualification and disclosure obligations and recommending references to statutory provisions for immunity.
